description_long: This is a vintage military biplane with an olive green fuselage and red star insignias on the wings and body, reminiscent of early to mid-20th century Soviet aircraft. The airplane features a high-mounted, straight upper wing and a slightly lower, parallel lower wing, both supported by struts and wires. The nose houses a single wooden propeller, and the engine cowling is painted in a slightly different shade, suggesting a radial piston engine. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer with the number '2' painted on it, and a horizontal stabilizer with bracing. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, typical of taildragger configurations. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage, likely accommodating a single pilot, and appears to be open or minimally enclosed.
description: A vintage olive green biplane with red star insignias, wooden propeller, and fixed taildragger landing gear, featuring a forward open cockpit and conventional tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the olive green fuselage, red star insignias, wooden propeller, and the number '2' on the tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual wings, central propeller, and the symmetrical structure of the landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same insignias and tail number, with clear visibility of the wing struts and tailwheel.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the alignment of the landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with straight, parallel wings supported by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; standard rudder and elevator surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open or minimally enclosed single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine cowling.
